---
title: "Safe & Maintainable AI Apps in Python"
subtitle: "SciPy 2026 Tutorial"
title-block-banner: false
---

::: {.hero}
![](slides/images/me-square.png){.hero-photo}

### Carson Sievert · Posit

A 4-hour hands-on tutorial on building safe, verifiable, and maintainable AI apps in Python — using **chatlas**, **shinychat**, and **querychat**.
:::

::: {.quick-links}
[GitHub Repo](https://github.com/cpsievert/scipy26-tutorial){.btn .btn-outline-secondary role="button"}
:::


## Slides

1. [Welcome & Setup](slides/00-welcome.html)
2. [Foundations](slides/01-foundations.html)
3. [Intro to chatting with LLMs (chatlas)](slides/02-basics.html)
4. [Recap](slides/03-recap.html)
5. [Tool calling and agents](slides/04-tools.html)
6. [Structured data extraction](slides/05-data.html)
7. [Chatbots](slides/06-chatbots.html)
8. [Recap & Sendoff](slides/07-recap.html)
9. [Evals](slides/08-chat-evals.html)
10. [Observability (OTel)](slides/09-chat-otel.html)
11. [querychat](slides/10-chat-query.html)
12. [Recap & Sendoff](slides/11-sendoff.html)

## Setup

A computing environment with all of the dependencies needed to run exercises is available [here](https://dev.workshop.posit.team). Please follow the instructions in the [welcome slides](slides/00-welcome.html) to get set up.

## Local Setup

If you'd rather locally run the tutorial exercises, follow the following instructions to get set up. 

1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/cpsievert/scipy26-tutorial.git
cd scipy26-tutorial
```

2. [Install uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/) globally

```bash
# macOS/Linux
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
```

```bash
# Windows
powershell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex"
```

If you can't use uv for some reason, Just do what you'd normally do to install the `requirements.txt`

3. Setup a virtual environment

```bash
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate
```

4. Install dependencies

```bash
uv pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## LLM access

Exercises depend on access to an LLM. Here are a few different ways to get access:

1. Sign up for a free trial at <https://posit.ai/>. No credit card required. You can use the free trial for the entire workshop.

2. Bring your own API key. If you have your own API key, like `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, etc. that will work just fine.

3. Run a local model on your laptop via **LM Studio** or **Ollama**. No key required, fully private, but slower and requires a beefy laptop. See the [LM Studio docs](https://lmstudio.io/docs/) for setup instructions.

For more choices and details, see https://posit-dev.github.io/chatlas/get-started/models.html

## Exercises and more

Can be found in the [GitHub repo](https://github.com/cpsievert/scipy26-tutorial). See the `exercises/` folder.

## Resources

- [chatlas docs](https://posit-dev.github.io/chatlas/)
- [shinychat docs](https://posit-dev.github.io/shinychat/py/)
- [Shiny GenAI chatbots guide](https://shiny.posit.co/py/docs/genai-chatbots.html)
- [querychat docs](https://posit-dev.github.io/querychat/py/index.html)
